title: Reza Hakimazar
text:
I am a master’s student in research psychology at the University of Hamburg, currently working under the supervision of Dr. Lennart Wittkuhn on developing Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable (FAIR) teaching materials.
My academic interests include social psychology 1, and science communication 2. Recently, I have also been focusing on improving my skills in quantitative research methods in psychology.

title: MORAL DECISION MAKING ACROSS CULTURES: INVESTIGATING THE EFFECTS OF STRESS
text:
Background


Stress is perceived and coped with differently across cultures (2003) and (2018).
However, there is little evidence about the effect of stress on moral decision-making across cultures.

title: Reza Hakimazar
text:
Background


Culture has a sizable influence on cognition, from color perception to attention and moral decision-making (Jameson 2005), (Ji and Yap 2016), and (Sachdeva, Singh, and Medin 2011).
Previous studies on moral decision-making show significant cultural differences, even in neural representations (Han, Glover, and Jeong 2014) and (Seo, Na, and Kim 2021).
One major factor that affects moral decision-making is stress (Shapiro, Jazaieri, and Goldin 2012), (Singer et al. 2017), and (Starcke et al. 2011)





Background


Stress is perceived and coped with differently across cultures (Hashim 2003) and (Vallejo et al. 2018).
However, there is little evidence about the effect of stress on moral decision-making across cultures.








What is moral decision making?



It a type of decisiom making within a moral context.





How codes analyze the data
فارسی مینویسم تا ببینم چگونه رندر میکند this is a new slide


click to see the code
# Load necessary libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Drift Diffusion Model Function
simulate_ddm <- function(n_trials, drift_rate, threshold, non_decision_time, noise) {
  decisions <- numeric(n_trials)
  reaction_times <- numeric(n_trials)
  
  for (i in 1:n_trials) {
    evidence <- 0
    rt <- non_decision_time
    while (abs(evidence) < threshold) {
      evidence <- evidence + rnorm(1, mean = drift_rate, sd = noise)
      rt <- rt + 0.001  # Increment time by 1ms
    }
    decisions[i] <- ifelse(evidence >= threshold, 1, 0)  # Decision: 1 = "Help others", 0 = "Personal Gain"
    reaction_times[i] <- rt
  }
  return(data.frame(Decisions = decisions, RT = reaction_times))
}

# Set parameters for two conditions: High moral conflict and Low moral conflict
n_trials <- 1000  # Number of simulated trials
condition_1 <- simulate_ddm(n_trials, drift_rate = 0.3, threshold = 1, non_decision_time = 0.2, noise = 0.1)
condition_2 <- simulate_ddm(n_trials, drift_rate = 0.1, threshold = 1, non_decision_time = 0.2, noise = 0.1)

# Add a column indicating condition
condition_1$Condition <- "High Conflict"
condition_2$Condition <- "Low Conflict"

# Combine datasets
ddm_data <- rbind(condition_1, condition_2)

# Calculate summary statistics
summary_stats <- ddm_data %>%
  group_by(Condition) %>%
  summarise(Accuracy = mean(Decisions), Mean_RT = mean(RT), SD_RT = sd(RT))
